stimulus: In her study of Willa Cather’s My Ántonia (1918), scholar Rosa Lindgren contends that the novel renders the Nebraska prairie as “a landscape both unforgiving and tenderly ______blank a fitting description given that Cather drew on her own childhood among Bohemian immigrant families on the Great Plains.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. The convention being tested is punctuation between a main clause and a supplementary noun phrase. The comma correctly marks the boundary between the main clause (“scholar...familiar”) and the supplementary noun phrase (“a fitting description”) that describes Lindgren’s reading of the novel.

Choice A is incorrect because a semicolon and the conjunction “and” can’t mark the boundary between a main clause and a supplementary noun phrase. Choice B is incorrect because joining the main clause to the following noun phrase with “and” produces an illogical sentence suggesting that the novel itself is “a fitting description.” Choice D is incorrect because it fails to mark the boundary between the main clause and the supplementary noun phrase with appropriate punctuation.
